data.body:
PASS 1 review — T10 (Infrastructure / IaC) [overlay]: PASS / not-applicable, no findings.

The T10 overlay applies only when the plan provisions or configures infrastructure (cloud resources; IaC via Terraform/CloudFormation/CDK/Pulumi/Ansible; Kubernetes/Helm). This epic and child story F do none of that — the work is entirely test-architecture and reconciler code:
- file_impact is a new in-repo test (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/test_roundtrip.py), a bash probe edit (scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh), and a docs page (docs/jira-sync-setup.md). No infra resources, state files, IAM policies, secrets material, or cluster manifests are created or configured.
- The "flag/canary" and "warn-first rollout" referenced in the parent's Rollout & rollback are application-level feature toggles for the sub-op invariant (story E), not infrastructure provisioning — they introduce no remote state, IAM grants, stateful resources, secrets, or cost-bearing infra.
- The "opt-in/scheduled live variant" (AC3) reuses the existing REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL + creds-gating test convention and documents auth preconditions; it provisions nothing.

Per the overlay's applicability gate ("else PASS not-applicable") and its ANTI-FP rule ("not-applicable for non-infra tickets"), none of the T10 binary checks (remote state/locking, least-privilege IAM, idempotency/drift, blast-radius/env isolation/prevent_destroy, plaintext secrets, cost/sizing, observability/teardown) is engaged. Clean for T10.
